docs.title: Dexterous Hand SDK
docs.text:
Welcome to the official SDK documentation for the Stonedrum Robotics dexterous hands.

Our dexterous hand series (including the Linkerbot L20 Lite, L20, and L30) provides unmatched precision and reliability for manipulation research, teleoperation, and embodied AI.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• High Degrees of Freedom: 20 to 21 DOF for biomimetic manipulation.
  • Native ROS 2 Support: Full integration with ROS 2 Humble, Iron, and Jazzy.
  • Python SDK: A clean, typed Python API for rapid prototyping.
  • Simulation Ready: Seamless integration with MuJoCo and MoveIt 2.
  • Hardware Integration: Standardized interfaces for UR and Franka Panda arms.

docs.title: Quick Install
docs.text: pip install dexterous-hand

docs.title: Compatibility
docs.text: Platform Supported Versions Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 ROS 2 Humble, Iron, Jazzy OS Ubuntu 22.04, Ubuntu 24.04

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Proceed to Getting Started to set up your environment.
  • Read the API Reference to explore the SDK.

docs.title: Driver API
docs.text:
Bases: ABC

Minimal interface implemented by vendor-specific drivers.

Source code in dexterous-hand-sdk/dexterous_hand/driver.py 
class HandDriver(ABC):
    """Minimal interface implemented by vendor-specific drivers."""

    @abstractmethod
    def connect(self) -> None:
        """Open communication with the hand."""

    @abstractmethod
    def disconnect(self) -> None:
        """Close communication with the hand."""

    @abstractmethod
    def read_joints(self) -> list[JointState]:
        """Return the current joint states."""

    @abstractmethod
    def command_positions(self, positions_rad: dict[str, float]) -> None:
        """Command target joint positions in radians."""

    @abstractmethod
    def stop(self) -> None:
        """Stop motion immediately where the hardware supports it."""

Bases: HandDriver

In-memory driver for tests, demos, and documentation examples.

Source code in dexterous-hand-sdk/dexterous_hand/driver.py 
class MockHandDriver(HandDriver):
    """In-memory driver for tests, demos, and documentation examples."""

    def __init__(self, joint_names: list[str]) -> None:
        self._connected = False
        self._positions = {name: 0.0 for name in joint_names}

    def connect(self) -> None:
        self._connected = True

    def disconnect(self) -> None:
        self._connected = False

    def read_joints(self) -> list[JointState]:
        self._require_connected()
        return [JointState(name=name, position_rad=value) for name, value in self._positions.items()]

    def command_positions(self, positions_rad: dict[str, float]) -> None:
        self._require_connected()
        unknown = set(positions_rad) - set(self._positions)
        if unknown:
            raise KeyError(f"Unknown joints: {sorted(unknown)}")
        self._positions.update(positions_rad)

    def stop(self) -> None:
        self._require_connected()

    def _require_connected(self) -> None:
        if not self._connected:
            raise RuntimeError("Driver is not connected")

docs.title: Finger API
docs.text:
A named finger with ordered joints and safety limits.

Source code in dexterous-hand-sdk/dexterous_hand/finger.py 
@dataclass
class Finger:
    """A named finger with ordered joints and safety limits."""

    name: str
    joint_names: list[str]
    lower_limits_rad: list[float]
    upper_limits_rad: list[float]

    def validate_positions(self, positions_rad: list[float]) -> None:
        """Validate joint positions against configured limits."""
        if len(positions_rad) != len(self.joint_names):
            msg = f"{self.name} expected {len(self.joint_names)} joints, got {len(positions_rad)}"
            raise ValueError(msg)

        for joint, value, lower, upper in zip(
            self.joint_names, positions_rad, self.lower_limits_rad, self.upper_limits_rad
        ):
            if value < lower or value > upper:
                msg = f"{joint} position {value:.3f} rad outside [{lower:.3f}, {upper:.3f}]"
                raise ValueError(msg)

State for a single actuated joint.

Source code in dexterous-hand-sdk/dexterous_hand/finger.py 
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class JointState:
    """State for a single actuated joint."""

    name: str
    position_rad: float
    velocity_rad_s: float = 0.0
    effort_nm: float = 0.0

docs.title: validate_positions(positions_rad)
docs.text:
Validate joint positions against configured limits.

Source code in dexterous-hand-sdk/dexterous_hand/finger.py 
def validate_positions(self, positions_rad: list[float]) -> None:
    """Validate joint positions against configured limits."""
    if len(positions_rad) != len(self.joint_names):
        msg = f"{self.name} expected {len(self.joint_names)} joints, got {len(positions_rad)}"
        raise ValueError(msg)

    for joint, value, lower, upper in zip(
        self.joint_names, positions_rad, self.lower_limits_rad, self.upper_limits_rad
    ):
        if value < lower or value > upper:
            msg = f"{joint} position {value:.3f} rad outside [{lower:.3f}, {upper:.3f}]"
            raise ValueError(msg)

docs.title: Grasp Library API
docs.text:
Library of common grasp primitives for demos and tests.

Source code in dexterous-hand-sdk/dexterous_hand/grasp_library.py 
class GraspLibrary:
    """Library of common grasp primitives for demos and tests."""

    def __init__(self, poses: dict[str, GraspPose] | None = None) -> None:
        self._poses = poses or self.default_poses()

    @staticmethod
    def default_poses() -> dict[str, GraspPose]:
        """Return conservative Cutkosky-inspired mock poses for initial demos."""
        return {
            "relaxed_open": GraspPose(
                "relaxed_open",
                {
                    "thumb_flex": 0.08,
                    "index_flex": 0.08,
                    "middle_flex": 0.08,
                    "ring_flex": 0.08,
                    "little_flex": 0.08,
                },
                "Slightly open rest posture for transport, inspection, and safe idle demos.",
            ),
            "precision_pinch": GraspPose(
                "precision_pinch",
                {
                    "thumb_flex": 0.65,
                    "index_flex": 0.7,
                    "middle_flex": 0.12,
                    "ring_flex": 0.08,
                    "little_flex": 0.08,
                },
                "Thumb and index fingertip contact for small objects such as pegs or beads.",
            ),
            "tripod": GraspPose(
                "tripod",
                {
                    "thumb_flex": 0.7,
                    "index_flex": 0.65,
                    "middle_flex": 0.62,
                    "ring_flex": 0.18,
                    "little_flex": 0.18,
                },
                "Three-point precision grasp for pens, markers, and small tools.",
            ),
            "lateral_pinch": GraspPose(
                "lateral_pinch",
                {
                    "thumb_flex": 0.72,
                    "index_flex": 0.38,
                    "middle_flex": 0.22,
                    "ring_flex": 0.18,
                    "little_flex": 0.18,
                },
                "Thumb pad against the side of the index finger for cards, keys, or tags.",
            ),
            "index_point": GraspPose(
                "index_point",
                {
                    "thumb_flex": 0.42,
                    "index_flex": 0.0,
                    "middle_flex": 0.82,
                    "ring_flex": 0.84,
                    "little_flex": 0.86,
                },
                "Index finger extended with other digits curled for buttons or touch targets.",
            ),
            "cylindrical_power": GraspPose(
                "cylindrical_power",
                {
                    "thumb_flex": 0.55,
                    "index_flex": 0.9,
                    "middle_flex": 0.9,
                    "ring_flex": 0.9,
                    "little_flex": 0.9,
                },
                "Power grasp wrapping all fingers around a cylindrical object or handle.",
            ),
            "spherical_power": GraspPose(
                "spherical_power",
                {
                    "thumb_flex": 0.62,
                    "index_flex": 0.78,
                    "middle_flex": 0.82,
                    "ring_flex": 0.78,
                    "little_flex": 0.72,
                },
                "Spread-finger power grasp for balls, knobs, and rounded objects.",
            ),
            "hook": GraspPose(
                "hook",
                {
                    "thumb_flex": 0.05,
                    "index_flex": 1.05,
                    "middle_flex": 1.08,
                    "ring_flex": 1.08,
                    "little_flex": 1.02,
                },
                "Fingers curled without thumb opposition for carrying handles or bags.",
            ),
            "platform": GraspPose(
                "platform",
                {
                    "thumb_flex": 0.18,
                    "index_flex": 0.05,
                    "middle_flex": 0.05,
                    "ring_flex": 0.05,
                    "little_flex": 0.05,
                },
                "Flat extended-finger support posture for tray or underside support tasks.",
            ),
            "open": GraspPose(
                "open",
                {},
                "Backward-compatible alias for an open relaxed posture.",
            ),
            "pinch": GraspPose(
                "pinch",
                {
                    "thumb_flex": 0.65,
                    "index_flex": 0.7,
                    "middle_flex": 0.12,
                    "ring_flex": 0.08,
                    "little_flex": 0.08,
                },
                "Backward-compatible alias for the precision_pinch grasp.",
            ),
            "cylindrical": GraspPose(
                "cylindrical",
                {
                    "thumb_flex": 0.55,
                    "index_flex": 0.9,
                    "middle_flex": 0.9,
                    "ring_flex": 0.9,
                    "little_flex": 0.9,
                },
                "Backward-compatible alias for the cylindrical_power grasp.",
            ),
        }

    def get(self, name: str) -> GraspPose:
        """Return a named grasp pose."""
        try:
            return self._poses[name]
        except KeyError as exc:
            raise KeyError(f"Unknown grasp pose: {name}") from exc

    def names(self) -> list[str]:
        """List available grasp names."""
        return sorted(self._poses)

docs.title: Hand API
docs.text:
User-facing API for commanding a dexterous hand.

Source code in dexterous-hand-sdk/dexterous_hand/hand.py 
class Hand:
    """User-facing API for commanding a dexterous hand."""

    def __init__(self, driver: HandDriver, grasp_library: GraspLibrary | None = None) -> None:
        self.driver = driver
        self.grasps = grasp_library or GraspLibrary()

    @classmethod
    def mock(cls) -> "Hand":
        """Create a mock hand suitable for examples and tests."""
        driver = MockHandDriver(DEFAULT_JOINTS)
        driver.connect()
        return cls(driver)

    def read_joints(self) -> list[JointState]:
        """Read current joint states."""
        return self.driver.read_joints()

    def move_joints(self, positions_rad: dict[str, float]) -> None:
        """Move joints to target positions."""
        self.driver.command_positions(positions_rad)

    def move_to_grasp(self, grasp_name: str) -> None:
        """Move to a named grasp pose."""
        pose = self.grasps.get(grasp_name)
        self.move_joints(pose.joint_positions_rad)

    def open(self) -> None:
        """Open the hand to a neutral pose."""
        joints = {state.name: 0.0 for state in self.read_joints()}
        self.move_joints(joints)

    def stop(self) -> None:
        """Stop motion immediately."""
        self.driver.stop()

docs.title: Teleoperation API
docs.text:
Maps teleoperation frames to hand commands.

Source code in dexterous-hand-sdk/dexterous_hand/teleoperation.py 
class TeleoperationController:
    """Maps teleoperation frames to hand commands."""

    def __init__(self, hand: Hand) -> None:
        self.hand = hand

    def apply_frame(self, frame: TeleoperationFrame) -> None:
        """Apply a normalized teleoperation command."""
        if not frame.joint_targets_rad:
            return
        self.hand.move_joints(frame.joint_targets_rad)

    def read_glove_frame(self) -> TeleoperationFrame:
        """Read a glove frame from a configured device."""
        raise NotImplementedError("Glove device integration is vendor-specific.")

Normalized teleoperation command frame.

Source code in dexterous-hand-sdk/dexterous_hand/teleoperation.py 
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TeleoperationFrame:
    """Normalized teleoperation command frame."""

    joint_targets_rad: dict[str, float]
    source: str = "unknown"

docs.title: apply_frame(frame)
docs.text:
Apply a normalized teleoperation command.

Source code in dexterous-hand-sdk/dexterous_hand/teleoperation.py 
def apply_frame(self, frame: TeleoperationFrame) -> None:
    """Apply a normalized teleoperation command."""
    if not frame.joint_targets_rad:
        return
    self.hand.move_joints(frame.joint_targets_rad)

    """Read a glove frame from a configured device."""
    raise NotImplementedError("Glove device integration is vendor-specific.")

docs.title: Education Course
docs.text:
The SDK is excellent for university curricula.

Suggested 1-Day Workshop: 1. Morning: Introduction to kinematics and the Python SDK (Tutorials 1 & 2). 2. Afternoon: Integration with vision systems and ROS 2 grasping pipelines. 3. Project: Programming the hand to play rock-paper-scissors using camera input.

docs.title: Imitation Learning
docs.text:
Imitation learning (IL) and reinforcement learning (RL) require robust data collection.

By using the teleoperation setup, you can record state-action trajectories. Our ROS 2 bags provide synchronized joint states, commands, and optional RGB-D camera feeds, streamlining the data pipeline for models like ACT or Diffusion Policies.

docs.title: Research & Manipulation
docs.text:
The Linkerbot series hands are designed for manipulation research.

  • High-DOF: Enables complex in-hand manipulation (e.g., object reorientation, pen spinning).
  • Sensors: Pressure sensors allow for tactile feedback and slip detection algorithms.
  • Datasets: The SDK is perfect for gathering large-scale grasping datasets.

docs.title: Teleoperation Rig
docs.text: Combining the dexterous hand with the EG Glove creates a 1:1 teleoperation rig.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Calibrate the EG Glove using the provided utility.
  2. Launch the teleoperation_node.
  3. The driver automatically handles coordinate mapping and scaling from human hand dimensions to the robotic hand.

docs.title: Control Modes
docs.text:
The SDK and firmware support multiple control paradigms to suit different research needs.

  • Position Control: Direct joint angle commands (default).
  • Torque Control: Commanded joint efforts, essential for compliant manipulation and reinforcement learning.
  • Impedance Control: Virtual spring-damper behavior at the joint level.

Check the driver documentation to see how to switch modes.

docs.title: Grasp Taxonomy
docs.text:
We support the Cutkosky grasp taxonomy for standardized manipulation tasks.

  • Power Grasps: Cylindrical, spherical, palmar.
  • Precision Grasps: Pinch, tripod, lateral.

You can trigger these directly using the Hand.move_to_grasp("pinch") method. See the GraspLibrary API for the full list of supported poses.

docs.title: Hand Model
docs.text:
The dexterous hand is modeled as a kinematic tree.

Each finger consists of multiple joints, typically covering flexion/extension and abduction/adduction.

graph LR
    Palm --> Thumb_Base
    Thumb_Base --> Thumb_Proximal
    Thumb_Proximal --> Thumb_Distal

Refer to the Finger API reference for the joint naming conventions and ranges.

docs.title: Safety Constraints
docs.text:
To protect both operators and hardware, several safety constraints are enforced at the driver level:

  • Position Limits: Hard stops in firmware prevent hyperextension.
  • Velocity Limits: Max joint velocities are clamped.
  • Torque/Current Limits: Overcurrent protection automatically shuts down the motor if an obstacle is hit.
  • Emergency Stop: The hardware includes an E-Stop circuit.

Note: CE certification status is currently under verification — please contact Stonedrum Robotics for the latest compliance documentation.

docs.title: Docker Deployment
docs.text:
We provide a Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml to isolate the ROS 2 environment.

cd dexterous-hand-sdk/docker
docker-compose up -d
docker-compose exec ros2_env bash

This ensures all dependencies (ROS 2, MoveIt 2, MuJoCo) are pre-installed.

docs.title: Realtime Performance
docs.text:
For high-frequency control (e.g., >500Hz impedance control), a realtime kernel is required.

  1. Install linux-image-rt on Ubuntu.
  2. Configure GRUB to boot into the RT kernel.
  3. Ensure the serial port latency timer is set to 1ms: bash setserial /dev/ttyUSB0 low_latency

docs.title: Démarrage Rapide
docs.text:
Ce guide vous permet de lancer votre premier mouvement en moins de 5 minutes.

Exécutez ce script Python :

from dexterous_hand import Hand

def main():
    hand = Hand.mock()
    hand.move_joints({"thumb_flex": 0.3, "index_flex": 0.4})
    for state in hand.read_joints():
        print(f"{state.name}: {state.position_rad:.2f} rad")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

docs.title: FAQ (Foire Aux Questions)
docs.text:
1. Le SDK est-il compatible avec MuJoCo ? Oui, des modèles XML sont fournis pour MuJoCo.

2. Comment contacter le support ? Envoyez un e-mail à info@stonedrum.co.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Pour installer le SDK :

pip install dexterous-hand

Si vous utilisez ROS 2 :

cd ~/ros2_ws/src
git clone https://github.com/stonedrum-robotics/dexterous-hand-sdk.git
cd ~/ros2_ws
colcon build --packages-select dexterous_hand_ros2

docs.title: First Movement
docs.text:
Get your hand moving in under 5 minutes with hardware connected, or 2 minutes in simulation.

Create a file named hello_hand.py:

from dexterous_hand import Hand

def main():
    # Use Hand.mock() for simulation, or Hand(driver=HardwareDriver()) for real hardware.
    hand = Hand.mock()

    print("Moving joints...")
    hand.move_joints({"thumb_flex": 0.3, "index_flex": 0.4})

    for state in hand.read_joints():
        print(f"{state.name}: {state.position_rad:.2f} rad")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

Run the script:

python3 hello_hand.py

Expected Output:

Moving joints...
thumb_flex: 0.30 rad
index_flex: 0.40 rad
...

docs.title: Hardware Setup
docs.text:
Follow these steps to safely unpack and connect your dexterous hand.

  1. Unboxing: Carefully remove the hand from the Pelican case. Do not apply force to the fingers during unboxing.
  2. Mounting: Use the provided adapter plate to mount the hand to your robot arm flange (e.g., ISO 9409-1-50-4-M6).
  3. Cabling: Connect the power and communication cables (CAN/RS485).
  4. Power Supply: Ensure the power supply is set to the correct voltage (24V or 48V depending on your model) before turning it on.

For detailed pinouts, refer to the Hardware Section.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
The SDK can be installed directly via pip. We recommend using a virtual environment.

# Create and activate a virtual environment
python3 -m venv dexenv
source dexenv/bin/activate

# Install the SDK
pip install dexterous-hand

docs.title: ROS 2 Setup
docs.text:
If you are using ROS 2, clone the repository into your workspace and build it:

cd ~/ros2_ws/src
git clone https://github.com/stonedrum-robotics/dexterous-hand-sdk.git
cd ~/ros2_ws
colcon build --packages-select dexterous_hand_ros2
source install/setup.bash

docs.title: Docker Option
docs.text: A docker-compose.yml file is provided for quick setup. See the Docker Deployment guide.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
A dexterous hand mimics the complexity of a human hand, enabling robots to perform fine manipulation tasks that standard parallel grippers cannot handle.

graph TD
    A[Robot Arm] --> B[Dexterous Hand]
    B --> C(Thumb)
    B --> D(Index Finger)
    B --> E(Middle Finger)
    B --> F(Ring Finger)
    B --> G(Little Finger)

By providing independent control over multiple joints across 5 fingers, the Linkerbot series hands unlock advanced use cases in imitation learning, teleoperation, and autonomous grasping.

Refer to the Hand API class for software control details.

docs.title: Accessories
docs.text:
• Adapter Plates: Custom aluminum plates for various ISO flanges.
  • Cables: High-flex cables for continuous robotic motion.
  • Power Supplies: Industrial 24V/48V DIN-rail power supplies.

docs.title: EG Glove
docs.text:
The EG Glove provides high-fidelity motion capture for teleoperation.

Connect via USB or Bluetooth. Ensure proper calibration before starting teleoperation nodes.

Note: CE certification status is currently under verification — please contact Stonedrum Robotics for the latest compliance documentation.

docs.title: Franka Panda Integration
docs.text:
Mounting to the Franka Panda:

  1. Mechanical: Ensure the payload (1.1kg for L20) is updated in the Franka Desk settings.
  2. Electrical: Connect to the external controller box.
  3. ROS 2: Use franka_ros2 alongside our dexterous_hand_ros2 nodes.

docs.title: Universal Robots (UR) Integration
docs.text:
Mounting the hand to a UR5e or UR10e:

  1. Mechanical: Use the standard ISO 9409-1-50-4-M6 adapter plate.
  2. Electrical: Route the tool cable through the UR arm or use external dressing.
  3. ROS 2: Launch the combined UR + Hand URDF for unified MoveIt 2 planning.

docs.title: Linkerbot L20
docs.text: The Linkerbot L20 is the flagship 21-DOF linkage-driven hand for research.

docs.title: Specifications
docs.text: Parameter Value DOF 21 (16 active + 5 coupled) Weight 1100g Load 10kg Communication CAN / RS485

docs.title: Wiring
docs.text:
Pin 1: VCC (24V)
Pin 2: GND
Pin 3: CAN H
Pin 4: CAN L

Note: CE certification status is currently under verification — please contact Stonedrum Robotics for the latest compliance documentation.

docs.title: Linkerbot L30
docs.text: The Linkerbot L30 offers high precision and tendon-driven mechanisms for medical and delicate manipulation.

docs.title: Specifications
docs.text:
Parameter Value Mechanism Tendon-driven Speed 440°/s Comm CAN FD

Note: CE certification status is currently under verification — please contact Stonedrum Robotics for the latest compliance documentation.

docs.title: Launch Files
docs.text:
Launch files simplify starting the hand nodes with the correct parameters.

ros2 launch dexterous_hand_ros2 hand.launch.py use_mock:=false port:=/dev/ttyUSB0

Parameters: - use_mock: Set to true for simulation/testing without hardware. - port: Serial port for the hardware driver. - baudrate: Communication speed.

docs.title: ROS 2 Nodes
docs.text:
• HandNode: The core driver node. It instantiates the Hand class, publishes /hand/joint_states, and subscribes to /hand/joint_commands.
  • TeleoperationNode: Interfaces with the EG Glove or other teleoperation devices, streaming joint targets.
  • GraspActionServer: Provides an Action interface to execute pre-defined grasps from the Grasp Library with feedback.

docs.title: ROS 2 Integration Overview
docs.text:
The Stonedrum Robotics ROS 2 packages expose the Python SDK over standard ROS interfaces.

graph TD
    HandNode[HandNode] -->|sensor_msgs/JointState| JS[/hand/joint_states/]
    CMD[/hand/joint_commands/] --> HandNode
    TeleopNode[TeleoperationNode] -->|glove_state| TeleopTopic[/teleop/glove_state/]
    GraspActionServer[GraspActionServer] <-- execute_grasp action --> Client

These nodes abstract hardware complexities and enable standard ROS tools like RViz and MoveIt 2.

docs.title: Topics, Services, and Actions
docs.text: Interface Type Name Message/Service Type Description Topic Pub /hand/joint_states sensor_msgs/msg/JointState Current hand state Topic Sub /hand/joint_commands sensor_msgs/msg/JointState Desired joint targets Action Server /hand/execute_grasp custom Action Executes a named grasp Service Server /hand/set_control_mode custom Srv Switches between Position/Torque

docs.title: v0.1.0
docs.text:
• Initial SDK release.
  • Support for MockHandDriver and hardware drivers.
  • ROS 2 basic nodes and action server.

docs.title: Contact Support
docs.text:
For technical support, integration consulting, or commercial inquiries:

Email: info@stonedrum.co

Company: Stonedrum Robotics

docs.title: FAQ
docs.text:
1. What is the difference between L20 and L20 Lite? The L20 Lite is lighter and more suited for educational purposes, while the L20 offers higher payload and industrial-grade linkage.

2. Is ROS 1 supported? We officially support ROS 2. ROS 1 support is community-driven.

3. How do I purchase a hand? Please contact us at info@stonedrum.co.

(More FAQs to be added as community questions arise.)

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Hand fails to connect: - Check power supply. - Ensure /dev/ttyUSB0 has the correct permissions (sudo usermod -aG dialout $USER).

ROS 2 Node crashes on start: - Verify that use_mock is correctly set if hardware is not attached.

docs.title: Basic Joint Control
docs.text:
This tutorial covers examples/01_basic_joint_control.py.

from dexterous_hand import Hand

def main() -> None:
    hand = Hand.mock()
    hand.move_joints({"thumb_flex": 0.3, "index_flex": 0.4})
    for state in hand.read_joints():
        print(f"{state.name}: {state.position_rad:.2f} rad")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

docs.title: Walkthrough
docs.text:
1. Initialization: We instantiate a mock hand which creates an in-memory mock driver.
  2. Commanding: move_joints takes a dictionary of joint names and target radians.
  3. Feedback: read_joints returns JointState objects which we print out.

Expected Output:

thumb_flex: 0.30 rad
index_flex: 0.40 rad
...

docs.title: Grasp Sequence
docs.text:
This tutorial covers examples/02_grasp_sequence.py.

from dexterous_hand import Hand
import time

def main() -> None:
    hand = Hand.mock()
    for grasp in ["open", "pinch", "cylindrical", "open"]:
        hand.move_to_grasp(grasp)
        print(f"Applied grasp: {grasp}")
        time.sleep(1.0)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

docs.title: Walkthrough
docs.text:
1. Sequence: We define a list of named grasps from the GraspLibrary.
  2. Execution: move_to_grasp automatically translates the name into joint targets.
  3. Safety: Starting and ending with "open" ensures safe transitions.

Expected Output:

Applied grasp: open
Applied grasp: pinch
Applied grasp: cylindrical
Applied grasp: open

docs.title: Teleoperation
docs.text:
This tutorial covers examples/03_teleoperation_keyboard.py.

It demonstrates how to map arbitrary inputs (like a keyboard or data glove) to joint targets in real-time.

  1. The TeleoperationInterface allows defining mappings from sensors to joints.
  2. In a loop, inputs are read, scaled, and sent as commands to the hand.

Please see the example script for the full implementation details.

docs.title: MoveIt 2 Integration
docs.text:
The dexterous hand provides a full URDF/SRDF configuration package for MoveIt 2.

To start the MoveIt 2 demo:

ros2 launch dexterous_hand_moveit_config demo.launch.py

This launches RViz where you can use the interactive markers to plan collision-free motions for the hand joints or the arm.

docs.title: MuJoCo Simulation
docs.text:
A high-fidelity MuJoCo XML model is provided for reinforcement learning.

See examples/05_sim_mujoco.py.

python3 examples/05_sim_mujoco.py

The model includes correct inertial properties, collision meshes, and actuator definitions suitable for training policies before hardware deployment.
